_doc: Object-distribution spec ONLY. Defines what objects can exist: per-material density/activity ranges and how often each material is chosen. Contains NO stratification and NO coupling fields by design: stratification is a runtime choice passed to generate_dataset (it cannot be expressed as static bounds), and coupling is expressed implicitly by how tight you make a material's ranges. Edit freely; the user owns this policy. material_id indexes config['mcgpu']['materials'] (1-based: id 1 = first listed = air, reserved for background).

n_objects._doc: Number of inserts per phantom, uniform integer in [min, max] inclusive.

size_mm._doc: Base half-size (sphere-equivalent radius) of an object, uniform in [min, max] mm. Per-axis aspect ratios apply on top. Keep max below the FOV-derived cap (see bounds_tools.suggest_bounds) or objects get rejected by the FOV guard.

aspect_ratio._doc: Per-axis multiplier on base size, uniform in [min, max], drawn independently per axis. Equal multipliers -> sphere/cylinder (degenerate); spread -> elongated. This is what makes 'ellipsoid contains sphere' explore the elongated regime.

cylinder_height_mm._doc: Height of elliptic cylinders, uniform in [min, max] mm.

background._doc: Whole volume filled with this first (air). Painted before all inserts so no voxel is left as vacuum (material_id 0 is rejected by VoxelGrid.validate). material_id 1 = first material in the config list.

insert_materials._doc: One entry per material an insert may use. For each: the density range and activity range sampled when that material is chosen, and relative_frequency (how often it is chosen, normalized internally over the materials listed here). 'Coupling' is implicit: tighten a material's activity range to make it effectively cold/hot; widen for decoupled brightness. material_id indexes config['mcgpu']['materials'] (id 2 = second listed, etc.). Using water (id 2) at low density is a valid lung surrogate (electron-density argument at 511 keV).

fov_margin_mm._doc: Inserts kept at least this far inside the transaxial radius limit, so objects (and their voxel corners) do not spill past the scanner's sensitive region.
